subject: grammar
title: Exercise — Past Simple / Past Perfect Simple / Past Perfect Continuous
instruction: Fill in the gaps with the past simple, past perfect simple or past perfect continuous of the verbs in brackets.
passage:
Last year my friends [1] (arrange) for us to try fire-walking, which is when you walk on hot coals. I [2] (always/be) fascinated by it and I [3] (hear) people say it was an unforgettable experience. I was very excited when I [4] (arrive) on the day, although beforehand I [5] (feel) a little frightened! My friends and I [6] (come) in the hope that by the end of the day we would be able to say we [7] (walk) across hot, burning coals.

Our teacher was very good, and by teatime we [8] (learn) a great deal and had [9] (prepare) the fires. I [10] (expect) to be terrified when the time came to walk, but as I [11] (take off) my shoes and socks I [12] (not/feel) afraid. I [13] (approach) the coals as all my friends before me [14] (do), and started walking! I could feel the heat, but as I [15] (step) back onto the grass on the other end I knew the coals [16] (not/burn) my feet at all. As I [17] (hope), all my friends [18] (manage) the walk and none of us were burnt. The whole experience was amazing, and I just wished I [19] (do) it sooner.

questions.text: Last year my friends ___ (arrange) for us to try fire-walking,
...and I ___ (hear) people say it was an unforgettable experience.
I was very excited when I ___ (arrive) on the day,
My friends and I ___ (come) in the hope that by the end of the day we would be able to say we 'd walked across hot, burning coals.
...we came in the hope that by the end of the day we would be able to say we ___ (walk) across hot, burning coals.
Our teacher was very good, and by teatime we ___ (learn) a great deal
I ___ (expect) to be terrified when the time came to walk,
...but as I ___ (take off) my shoes and socks
I ___ (approach) the coals as all my friends before me had done,
I approached the coals as all my friends before me ___ (do), and started walking!
...but as I ___ (step) back onto the grass on the other end
I knew the coals ___ (not/burn) my feet at all.
As I ___ (hope), all my friends managed the walk
...all my friends ___ (manage) the walk and none of us were burnt.
I just wished I ___ (do) it sooner.
